meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no design skills (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no design skills. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Antigravity Project Template: Build Pro Apps Without Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop letting poor design hold back your portfolio. Learn how to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to ship polished, professional web apps.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity project template bridges the gap between raw code and polished UI by providing pre-styled, industry-standard components. By pairing it with Claude Code, you offload design decisions to AI, allowing you to focus on logic while ensuring your student portfolio projects look like professional, high-end SaaS products.
content.intro:
You have the logic, the database schema, and the ambition, but the moment you open your browser, the UI feels amateur. This is the 'Designer’s Gap'—the silent killer of student portfolios. You spend 80% of your time fighting CSS margins instead of building the features that actually get you hired.

But the rules of the game have changed. You no longer need to be a designer to ship world-class software. By leveraging the Antigravity project template, you gain a professional design system out of the box, and by pairing it with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you can transform your functional MVP into a visual powerhouse in hours, not weeks.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Foundation
steps.description: Clone the template to establish a robust, responsive design system. This eliminates the 'blank canvas' anxiety and sets your project's visual hierarchy.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard template with Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons pre-configured.
steps.claude_code_command: git clone https://github.com/antigravity/template my-project && cd my-project

steps.title: Define Your Data Model
steps.description: Before styling, define your core entities. Claude Code excels at generating schema-aware components when the data structure is clear.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a Task Management app with User, Project, and Task models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema for a task management app'

steps.title: Component-First UI Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to map your data models to Antigravity components, ensuring consistency across your dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive dashboard view using Antigravity layout components and the Task model data.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'build a responsive dashboard using Antigravity components'

steps.title: Injecting State and Interactivity
steps.description: Connect your UI to logic. Antigravity handles the layout constraints, while you focus on the functional state transitions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add state management for task completion toggles using React hooks and Antigravity's button styles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add react state management for task completion toggles'

steps.title: Final Visual Polish
steps.description: Use AI-driven refinement to ensure your app meets accessibility standards and looks professional across mobile devices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the layout for mobile responsiveness and apply Antigravity spacing utilities to fix visual inconsistencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'fix mobile responsiveness and apply consistent spacing'

pro_tips: Treat your CSS variables as your single source of truth; never hardcode colors or spacing values.
Use Claude Code to audit your component accessibility (ARIA labels) to make your portfolio look 'senior' to recruiters.
Always generate your README.md using the same AI context you used to build the app; it ensures documentation accuracy.
Leverage the Antigravity 'component-first' approach to minimize prop-drilling by keeping logic local to the UI unit.
Run a final 'code review' prompt with Claude to identify redundant re-renders before deploying to Vercel.
content.next_steps: After finishing your first build, deploy it via Vercel, write a LinkedIn post detailing the technical challenges you solved, and start your next project using a more complex schema.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped with the same tools top-tier engineers use to ship products at scale. Stop worrying about design and start focusing on the architecture that will define your career. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
